event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a dark, purple-hued title screen for 'Ökko PHOENIX' and 'phoenix-animation.com', accompanied by whooshing and orchestral sounds, transitioning into a fast-paced montage of animated characters, including one with spiky hair holding a fan, a purple-haired character looking annoyed, and a green-haired character making a heart gesture, all set to an upbeat pop/rock song about a 'magic stack of cards' and things going wrong, leading to a chaotic scene of a blue car crashing through a wall and a glowing '13 CARDS' title. The scene shifts to a cozy living room with a gentle string melody, where a teal-haired character, Wadu, peeks from behind a door, walks in, picks up a blue remote, and turns on a retro TV displaying a pixelated game, emitting electronic beeps and clicks. Wadu smiles and sits on a pink couch as yellow-haired Spade, green-haired character, and purple-haired character appear, looking annoyed. Wadu sighs, and a male voice asks, "What you watching?", prompting a gasp/scream from Wadu, who looks downcast while the others maintain stern expressions. The green-haired character, with a mischievous grin and spiral glasses, watches as Wadu cheerfully exclaims, "Time to rock and roll!" while turning on the retro TV with the blue remote, which displays a pixelated game. Wadu appears dejected, letting out a frustrated "Ah!". The purple-haired character, with a stern look, stands behind the pink couch, holding the blue remote, and declares, "No! We're not watching this crap today!". Wadu protests, "But it's my turn today!", to which the purple-haired character retorts, "Wadu, we never took turns!". A brief struggle for the remote ensues with grunts and TV static. The green-haired character, with a smug expression, taunts Spade with, "Oh, Spade, give it to me. You never watch TV anyway." Spade, leaning on the couch, responds, "I am tonight. They're showing the 16th-century Ottoman-Hungarian War on Epoch TV," as dramatic historical music begins, and Spade excitedly urges, "Oh, turn it up!". Wadu groans, "Uh, not more power struggles." The green-haired character sarcastically adds, "We watch TV to become stupider, not smarter!" Spade defends his choice, "There are conky buns in this!" The purple-haired character warns, "Spade, don't give him the remote! Or he'll put on his soap operas for housewives!" Spade clarifies, "They're not just for housewives, but also for divorced men in their thirties." In the kitchen, a new brown-haired character, Fyodor, wearing a yellow shirt with a black collar and blue pants, opens a mostly empty refrigerator, looking disappointed and angry, hesitantly muttering "Uh..." before suggesting, "What about a cooking show?" The purple-haired character, still holding the blue remote, sternly tells Fyodor, "Just keep your calm, Fyodor. You can watch it later!" The scene shifts back to the living room where the purple-haired character, holding the blue remote, stands sternly behind the pink couch. The brown-haired character excitedly exclaims, "Oh yeah! My favorite cartoons are on today!", then struggles as the purple-haired character uses the blue remote to pour a dark liquid into his mug. The brown-haired character reacts with shock and disgust, letting out a despairing "Nooooo!" finding the refrigerator empty, leading to a frustrated and angry expression, holding a piece of paper, possibly a note or receipt, while the purple-haired character watches dismissively, uttering "Oops." The brown-haired character covers his face in despair, letting out a cartoonish scream of "Wah-hoo!" as the purple-haired character dismissively states, "Who cares? It's just comics. You'll make more." The scene shifts to a framed drawing titled 'Thirteen Cards,' which the brown-haired character gestures toward, becoming agitated and questioning, "More? More?" The green-haired character enters, smirking and raising a hand, declaring, "Need I remind you that I'm here to get stupider!" The yellow-haired character looks on with concern as the brown-haired character sighs, "Just... why bother?" and challenges the purple-haired character, "You think making comics is easy?" to which the purple-haired character dryly retorts, "Is it not?" The purple-haired character then lectures on comic books as "a primitive form of entertainment for the peasants." The teal-haired character laments, "I just wanted to watch some cartoons," prompting the brown-haired character to rally, "Exactly! Do you hear what our spongy friend said? Cartoons are cool! Comics are..." The purple-haired character challenges, "So you're saying you'll do better than me?" The brown-haired character, smiling mischievously and holding a piece of paper and a backpack, reveals, "We're literally your clones. You've literally called us up for this," as the purple-haired character begins to respond, "Well, if it's that..." before being cut off. A male voice, identified as the green-haired character, declares, "Then all of you will make a comic each! The best one, in my opinion, will choose what we're gonna watch," followed by his own laughter and a mischievous, "Who needs a remote!" as a pouring sound accompanies a dark liquid being dispensed from the blue remote, with the green-haired character adding, "Good luck!" and "Oh, well that'll keep him busy till the evening." The scene transitions to the living room, where the purple-haired character stands sternly behind the pink couch, holding the blue remote, while Spade leans on the couch with a neutral expression and Wadu sits dejectedly. The green-haired character, standing with a smug look, raises a hand. Footsteps and paper rustling are heard as the brown-haired character enters, reacting with a gasp of shock to the dark liquid poured into a mug, with upbeat, whimsical background music beginning. The brown-haired character excitedly exclaims, "I want this one and this one! Oh, this one's pretty," before finding an empty refrigerator and holding a paper, asking with frustration, "Uh, where's all the paper?" Spade chimes in, "You can have toilet paper!", followed by Wadu's exclamation, "Water!" The brown-haired character declares, "I thought of a masterpiece! And for that, I need a lot of paper!" The green-haired character interjects, "You're not gonna need these anymore!" accompanied by a tearing sound. Wadu states, "Oh, I'll do something much, much better," and is later seen in the kitchen, looking distressed while holding a purple pencil, pondering, "Hmm, comic books, comic books. What kinds are there?" He considers safety comics as "No, too boring," and caricatures like in Punch magazine as "No, too vulgar." The group gathers around a table, with the green-haired character laughing and pointing playfully at Spade, who looks distressed. The brown-haired character holds up a sign reading 'CARTOONS ARE COOL,' adding a mischievous tone. The video concludes with illustrated panels: one showing construction site safety rules with characters in hard hats, and another depicting a chaotic, surreal scene with exaggerated expressions and a speech bubble asking, 'Are we now in some alternative universe, where irony is king?' The final frame shows the group in a chaotic living room, with the brown-haired character holding a phone and Spade crawling on the floor, while the purple-haired character looks on sternly.

Yellow-haired character.description: A character with short yellow hair, wearing a yellow shirt with a red emblem, who leans on the couch with a neutral expression, later looking distressed and crawling on the floor, and speaks with a male voice, offering "You can have toilet paper!"

Pink couch.description: A large, plush pink couch with tassels, serving as the central seating area where the Teal-haired character sits dejectedly and the Yellow-haired character leans on it.

Purple-haired character.description: A character with spiky purple hair, wearing a dark blue shirt, who stands behind the couch with a stern, unimpressed look, holding the Blue remote, later maintaining a stern expression.

Retro TV.description: A vintage-style television with two antennas, displaying a pixelated game with a robot and a flower-like object, later showing a different game with a character and a castle.

Teal-haired character.description: A character with teal hair, wearing a brown scarf and a tan shirt, who sits dejectedly on the Pink couch, later appearing distressed in the kitchen while holding a purple pencil, and speaks with a thoughtful male voice, exclaiming "Water!", then deciding "Oh, I'll do something much, much better," and pondering comic book ideas, dismissing "safety" and "caricatures" as "too boring" or "too vulgar."

Blue remote.description: A small, blue handheld remote control used by the Purple-haired character to pour a dark liquid into a mug, later held firmly as the character maintains a stern demeanor.

Green-haired character.description: A character with curly green hair, wearing a blue shirt with a green spiral design, who stands with a smug expression, raising a hand, later laughing and pointing playfully, and speaks with a confident, mischievous male voice, instructing the group to make comics, declaring "Who needs a remote!", and later stating "You're not gonna need these anymore!" before a tearing sound.

Living room.description: A brightly colored, cozy room with orange walls, a kitchen area in the background, and various decorations including a dreamcatcher and a framed picture, serving as the main setting for the interactions.

Brown-haired character.description: A character with brown hair, wearing a yellow shirt with a black collar and blue pants, who reacts with a gasp of shock to a dark liquid, finds an empty refrigerator, holds a paper, and later holds a sign reading 'CARTOONS ARE COOL,' expressing excitement with "I want this one and this one! Oh, this one's pretty," frustration with "Uh, where's all the paper?", and determination with "I thought of a masterpiece! And for that, I need a lot of paper!"

Sign 'CARTOONS ARE COOL'.description: A white sign with green text held by the Brown-haired character, indicating a playful or defiant message, possibly related to the group's activities or mood.

Framed drawing 'Thirteen Cards'.description: A whimsical, hand-drawn illustration titled 'Thirteen Cards' featuring various fantastical characters like Felice, Deorlaf, Gábríel, Spadu, Rōma, Dústmäeg, Brēol, and Weard, displayed on the wall and gestured toward by the Brown-haired character.

Construction site safety rules panel.description: An illustrated panel titled 'Construction site safety rules' showing the characters in construction gear, including hard hats and orange vests, with one character holding a heart-shaped helmet.

Chaotic surreal scene panel.description: An illustrated panel depicting a surreal, chaotic scene with exaggerated expressions, featuring characters in various states of distress and excitement, with a speech bubble asking, 'Are we now in some alternative universe, where irony is king?'

raw_segments: The video opens with a dark, purple-hued title screen displaying 'Ökko PHOENIX' with a fiery animation and the website 'phoenix-animation.com', accompanied by whooshing and dramatic orchestral sounds. This transitions into a fast-paced montage of animated characters, including one with spiky hair holding a fan, another with purple hair looking annoyed, and a character with green hair making a heart gesture, all set to an upbeat pop/rock song with female vocals singing about a 'magic stack of cards' and how 'everything went wrong'. A chaotic scene with a blue car crashing through a wall leads to a glowing '13 CARDS' title. The scene then shifts to a cozy, colorful living room, where the music softens to a gentle, melancholic string melody. A cheerful teal-haired character peeks from behind a door, walks into the room, picks up a blue remote, and turns on a retro TV, which emits electronic beeps and clicks as it displays a pixelated game. The teal-haired character smiles, then sits on a plush pink couch as three other characters—one with yellow hair, one with green hair, and one with spiky purple hair—appear, looking annoyed or unimpressed. The teal-haired character sighs, then a male voice asks, "What you watching?" prompting a female gasp/scream from the teal-haired character, who then looks downcast while the others maintain their stern expressions.

The scene opens with a close-up of the Green-haired character, who has a mischievous grin and green spiral glasses. Electronic game sounds fill the air as the Teal-haired character, Wadu, cheerfully exclaims, "Time to rock and roll!" while turning on the Retro TV with the Blue remote. The TV displays a pixelated game featuring a robot and a flower-like object, but Wadu soon appears dejected, letting out a frustrated "Ah!" The Purple-haired character, with a stern, unimpressed look, stands behind the Pink couch, holding the Blue remote, and firmly declares, "No! We're not watching this crap today!" Wadu protests, "But it's my turn today!" to which the Purple-haired character retorts, "Wadu, we never took turns!" A brief struggle for the remote ensues, accompanied by grunts and TV static. The Green-haired character then stands with a smug expression, raising a hand, taunting the Yellow-haired character, Spade, with "Oh, Spade, give it to me. You never watch TV anyway." Spade, leaning on the couch with a neutral to slightly annoyed expression, responds with a calm, interested tone, "I am tonight. They're showing the 16th-century Ottoman-Hungarian War on Epoch TV," as dramatic historical music begins. Spade then excitedly urges, "Oh, turn it up!" Wadu, still dejected, groans, "Uh, not more power struggles." The Green-haired character, now sitting on the couch with a smug look, sarcastically adds, "We watch TV to become stupider, not smarter!" Spade defends his choice, "There are conky buns in this!" The Purple-haired character, still stern, warns, "Spade, don't give him the remote! Or he'll put on his soap operas for housewives!" Spade clarifies, "They're not just for housewives, but also for divorced men in their thirties." The scene shifts to the kitchen area, where a new Brown-haired character, Fyodor, wearing a yellow shirt with a black collar and blue pants, opens the refrigerator, revealing a mostly empty fridge. He looks disappointed and then angry, hesitantly muttering "Uh..." before suggesting, "What about a cooking show?" The scene returns to the living room, where the Purple-haired character, still holding the Blue remote, sternly tells Fyodor, "Just keep your calm, Fyodor. You can watch it later!" The overall atmosphere is one of mild tension and disappointment, with the characters' expressions and body language reflecting their emotions in the brightly colored, cozy yet slightly chaotic living room.

The Brown-haired character, now smiling mischievously and holding a piece of paper and a backpack, reveals a crucial detail: "We're literally your clones. You've literally called us up for this," suggesting a shift in mood or plan, as the Purple-haired character begins to respond, "Well, if it's that..." before being cut off.

A male voice, later identified as the Green-haired character, declares, "Then all of you will make a comic each! The best one, in my opinion, will choose what we're gonna watch," followed by his own laughter and a mischievous "Who needs a remote!" as a pouring sound accompanies a dark liquid being dispensed. What kinds are there?" He considers, "Ah, there's some about safety, like in school," but dismisses it as "No, too boring," and similarly rejects "caricatures like in Punch magazine?" as "No, too vulgar." The group gathers around a table, with the Green-haired character laughing and pointing playfully at the Yellow-haired character, who looks distressed. The video concludes with a series of illustrated panels: one showing construction site safety rules with the characters in hard hats, and another depicting a chaotic, surreal scene with exaggerated expressions and a speech bubble asking, 'Are we now in some alternative universe, where irony is king?' The final frame shows the group in a chaotic living room, with the Brown-haired character holding a phone and the Yellow-haired character crawling on the floor, while the Purple-haired character looks on sternly.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins in a chaotic, surreal scene featuring the Teal-haired character looking distressed, the Green-haired character smirking, and the Yellow-haired character screaming, set against a backdrop of a framed drawing 'Thirteen Cards' and a 'Construction site safety rules' panel, with a male voice, likely the Brown-haired character, dismissing a suggestion with "Memes? Uh, still no." The scene shifts to a dark, intense moment where the Purple-haired character, with glowing yellow eyes and a menacing expression, wields a sword amidst swirling blue energy, accompanied by a deep, guttural growl and whooshing sounds, before confidently declaring, "Yes, I'll have the best graphic novel." The setting transitions to a brightly lit kitchen where the Purple-haired character, now normal, stands sternly with arms crossed while the Teal-haired character dejectedly holds a purple pencil and a blank sheet of paper; the Brown-haired character enters, frustrated, stating, "If I could learn how to draw." The Teal-haired character anxiously expresses, "I can't decide. What if Yura doesn't like it?" followed by a high-pitched scream and "That's such a huge responsibility!" Both the Brown-haired and Purple-haired characters then state, "I need a partner." The Brown-haired character, in a different room with a box of crayons and a ruler, overwhelmed by creative tasks amidst pencil scribbling sounds, advises, "It's important to break the work into stages. For starters, I must collect more data," but sighs, "Hmm. This is still not enough," interrupted by loud mechanical grinding. The video opens in a brightly colored living room with orange walls and a pink couch, where the Teal-haired character, distressed, asks, "Is something wrong? Should I fix anything?" A menacing voice, likely the Purple-haired character, commands, "Hmm, give me that pencil." The Green-haired character smugly laughs, "Hahaha," while the Yellow-haired character, annoyed, asks, "Okay, I see. Can I get back to work?" Distressed whimpering, grunts, and animalistic noises are heard, possibly from the Brown-haired character, with a gruff voice interjecting, "Huh? Uh..." Upbeat, arcade-like electronic music plays as the Green-haired character exclaims, "Oh, that could be good!" followed by a loud impact, a grunt, and the Yellow-haired character screaming again. Dramatic orchestral music builds tension with sounds of quill writing, a sword being drawn, and a whoosh, as the Purple-haired character appears menacing with glowing yellow eyes and a sword, then returns to a stern demeanor in the kitchen, while the Brown-haired character looks overwhelmed at a whiteboard. The Green-haired character enters a room with papers, laughing, "Hee hee hee hee hee!" and draws on a comic strip with a green pencil, adding details to a character's face, smiling mischievously as the comic strip features a character with a surprised expression and a speech bubble saying 'Это что, коробка с призами?' (What is this, a box with prizes?). The final scene returns to the living room where the Teal-haired character sits on the Pink couch, softly asking, "Um, like this? Do you like it?" as she shows a drawing to the Yellow-haired character, who, holding a mug, appears annoyed and dismisses it with, "Hmm, yeah, it's fine."

raw_segments: The video opens with a chaotic, surreal scene featuring multiple characters, including the Teal-haired character looking distressed, the Green-haired character smirking, and the Yellow-haired character screaming, set against a backdrop of a framed drawing titled 'Thirteen Cards' and a panel labeled 'Construction site safety rules'. A male voice, likely the Brown-haired character, dismisses a suggestion with "Memes? Uh, still no." The scene abruptly shifts to a dark, intense moment where the Purple-haired character, now with glowing yellow eyes and a menacing expression, wields a sword amidst swirling blue energy, accompanied by a deep, guttural growl and whooshing sounds, suggesting a transformation or power surge. A confident male voice, likely the Purple-haired character, then declares, "Yes, I'll have the best graphic novel." The setting transitions to a brightly lit kitchen where the Purple-haired character, now back to normal, stands sternly with arms crossed while the Teal-haired character sits at the table, dejectedly holding a purple pencil and a blank sheet of paper. The Brown-haired character enters, holding a paper and looking frustrated, stating thoughtfully, "If I could learn how to draw." The Teal-haired character expresses anxiety with a distressed female voice, "I can't decide. What if Yura doesn't like it?" followed by a high-pitched scream and her exclaiming, "That's such a huge responsibility!" Both the Brown-haired and Purple-haired characters then state, "I need a partner." The Brown-haired character later appears in a different room, holding a box of crayons and a ruler, seemingly overwhelmed by creative tasks, as pencil scribbling sounds are heard. He calmly advises, "It's important to break the work into stages. For starters, I must collect more data," but then sighs, "Hmm. This is still not enough," amidst more scribbling. Loud mechanical grinding sounds briefly interrupt. The final scene returns to a living room where the Teal-haired character sits on the Pink couch, looking sad, and softly asks, "Um, like this? Do you like it?" as she shows a drawing to the Yellow-haired character, who, now holding a mug, appears annoyed and dismissive, responding with a curt, "Hmm, yeah, it's fine." The atmosphere shifts from surreal chaos to domestic tension, highlighting emotional struggles and creative frustration among the characters.

The video opens in a brightly colored living room with orange walls and a pink couch, where the Teal-haired character, appearing distressed while holding a purple pencil and a blank sheet of paper, asks in a concerned voice, "Is something wrong? Should I fix anything?" A deep, menacing voice, likely belonging to the Purple-haired character, then commands, "Hmm, give me that pencil." The Green-haired character stands smugly, raising a hand, and laughs, "Hahaha." The Yellow-haired character, initially screaming in distress, then sits on the pink couch, holding a mug and looking annoyed, stating, "Okay, I see. Can I get back to work?" This is followed by a loud impact sound and a grunt, and the Yellow-haired character screams again. Dramatic, orchestral music builds tension, accompanied by the sounds of a quill writing, a sword being drawn, and a whoosh. During this, the Purple-haired character appears menacing with glowing yellow eyes and a sword, then returns to a stern demeanor in the kitchen, while the Brown-haired character is seen in a different room, looking at a whiteboard with drawings, appearing overwhelmed. The Green-haired character enters a room with a stack of papers, laughing mischievously, "Hee hee hee hee hee!", and begins drawing on a comic strip with a green pencil, adding details to a character's face. The final shot shows the Green-haired character smiling mischievously as they continue drawing, with the comic strip featuring a character with a surprised expression and a speech bubble saying 'Это что, коробка с призами?' (What is this, a box with prizes?).

event_blocks.summary: The Green-haired character, with curly green hair and a blue shirt, mischievously draws on a comic strip amidst pencil scratching and 'boing' sounds, prompting the Yellow-haired character, in bed, to question if he's sleeping in Fyodor's bed or stealing art. The scene dramatically shifts to the Purple-haired character, with spiky purple hair and glowing yellow eyes, wielding a sword in a dark, purple-hued environment, declaring, 'I receive power that our father never dreamed of,' only to be challenged by a gravelly voice stating, 'He was never your father,' leading to the Purple-haired character's surprised 'Nani!' as their swords cross. In a kitchen, the Purple-haired character, now in a dark blue shirt, smirks at the distressed Teal-haired character, who asks how things are going, to which the Purple-haired character responds with satisfaction, 'Ooh, this is great. Just how I imagined it.' This is interrupted by the loud, booming voice of the Brown-haired character, Gabriel, exclaiming 'HOME!' and 'The deadline! Quick!' The scene shifts to a brightly lit living room where the Yellow-haired character, on a Pink couch, announces, 'Looky who I found,' identifying Gabriel. The Green-haired character asks about his origin, and a chorus of voices calls out 'Gabriel!' Gabriel appears worried in a cityscape, then is startled by a pigeon in a park, accompanied by cooing and squawking, while an elderly woman smiles beside a bench with another pigeon. Later, the Brown-haired character, in an orange room, is frustrated as a male voice exclaims, 'You're just in time!' He confronts the Yellow-haired character holding a tablet with character customization, who excitedly reveals, 'Better than that. Look!' The Brown-haired character takes the tablet, and the Yellow-haired character looks surprised. The Green-haired character, in a blue shirt with a green spiral, proudly declares, 'I made an animation!' when asked why it's only three frames, shouting defensively, 'Do you know how hard it is to make cartoons?!' The Brown-haired character watches sternly. In a doorway, the Brown-haired character holds papers between the Yellow-haired character and a concerned Pink-haired character, attempting to calm them. The Green-haired character waves dismissively, declaring, 'Move over. King in the castle.' The Brown-haired character's hand is shown with a pink, pixelated substance, which he drops, scattering papers, exclaiming, 'Oh wow, this is beautiful!' but is questioned if he copied the first Google result for manga, reluctantly admitting to using the second. The Purple-haired character, with glowing yellow eyes and a menacing expression, appears and confronts the Brown-haired character. The scene transitions to a dark, stormy mountain landscape where the Purple-haired character, in purple armor, stands triumphantly with a sword as a winged creature flies overhead, while voices discuss who's next and restrict internet access. Back in the room, the Purple-haired character glares at a terrified Brown-haired character. A dramatic narration about a fearless knight is interrupted by a critique: 'It's kinda generic.' The Brown-haired character, overwhelmed, holds a ruler and a box of crayons in an office space, as voices critique the story as 'corny' and lacking character motivation. The video ends with the Brown-haired character standing in a gray, overcast cityscape, wearing a blue jacket with a fur-lined hood, looking shocked and distressed.

raw_segments: The video opens with the Green-haired character, with curly green hair and a blue shirt, smiling mischievously while drawing on a comic strip, accompanied by pencil scratching and playful 'boing' sounds. The Yellow-haired character, lying in bed, questions him, asking in a casual tone, 'Are you sleeping in Fyodor's bed?' and 'Are you stealing someone else's art?'. The scene dramatically shifts to the Purple-haired character, with spiky purple hair and glowing yellow eyes, wielding a sword in a dark, purple-hued environment, declaring in a deep, resonant voice, 'I receive power that our father never dreamed of.' This is immediately challenged by a deeper, gravelly voice stating, 'He was never your father,' to which the Purple-haired character exclaims 'Nani!' in surprise as a narrator describes 'their swords cross.' The setting then changes to a kitchen where the Purple-haired character, now in a dark blue shirt, smirks at the distressed Teal-haired character, who asks 'So, how's it going?' The Purple-haired character responds with satisfaction, 'Ooh, this is great. Just how I imagined it,' to which the Teal-haired character modestly replies, 'Oh, don't mention it. I'm not...' before being cut off by the loud, booming voice of the Brown-haired character, Gabriel, exclaiming 'HOME!' and urgently adding, 'The deadline! Quick!' The scene shifts to a brightly lit living room where the Yellow-haired character, now sitting on a Pink couch, announces with a chuckle, 'Looky who I found,' identifying the Brown-haired character as 'Gabriel.' The Green-haired character asks 'Who is that?' and 'Where was he?' before a chorus of voices calls out 'Gabriel!' The Brown-haired character, Gabriel, is then shown looking worried in a cityscape, before the final scene shows him startled by a pigeon in a park, accompanied by distinct pigeon cooing and squawking, while an elderly woman smiles beside a bench with another pigeon.

The video opens with the Brown-haired character, wearing a yellow shirt and blue pants, looking frustrated and clenching his fists in an orange room, as a male voice exclaims, "You're just in time!" He confronts the Yellow-haired character, who is smiling while holding a tablet displaying a character customization screen. A male voice asks, "Did you finish the comic?" to which another male voice, likely the Yellow-haired character, excitedly replies, "Better than that. Look!" The Brown-haired character takes the tablet, and the Yellow-haired character’s expression shifts to surprise. The scene cuts to the Green-haired character, now in a blue shirt with a green spiral, drawing on a comic strip with a mischievous grin. A male voice, likely the Brown-haired character, questions, "Um, what is that?" The Green-haired character, with a proud male voice, declares, "I made an animation!" When asked why it's "only three frames," he shouts defensively, "Do you know how hard it is to make cartoons?!" The Brown-haired character watches with a stern expression. The scene shifts to a doorway where the Brown-haired character, now holding a stack of papers, stands between the Yellow-haired character and a new Pink-haired character, both looking concerned. The Brown-haired character's male voice attempts to calm the situation, saying, "All right, all right, all right." The Green-haired character reappears, waving dismissively, and a male voice, likely his, declares, "Move over. King in the castle." Suddenly, the Brown-haired character’s hand is shown with a pink, pixelated substance, which he drops, causing the papers to scatter. A male voice, likely the Brown-haired character, exclaims, "Oh wow, this is beautiful!" but is immediately challenged by another male voice, likely the Green-haired character, asking, "Did you copy the first result for manga on Google?" The first voice defensively replies, "What? No!" The challenging voice then asks, "The second?" to which the defensive voice reluctantly admits, "Yeah." A dramatic sound effect accompanies the Purple-haired character, now with glowing yellow eyes and a menacing expression, appearing in the room and confronting the Brown-haired character. The scene transitions to a dark, stormy mountain landscape where the Purple-haired character, now in a purple armored suit, stands triumphantly with a sword, as a large, winged creature flies overhead. During this, a male voice asks, "Who's next?" followed by a loud "Whoa!" A deep, authoritative male voice declares, "I think I'll restrict your internet access," and another male voice states, "That's enough. It's our turn." The scene cuts back to the room, where the Purple-haired character, now back in his dark blue shirt, glares at the Brown-haired character, who looks terrified. A deep, dramatic male voice with an echo effect begins a narration, "Get ready to read the most exciting saga about a fearless knight whose heroic exploits are glorified by mankind," only to be interrupted by another male voice stating, "It's kinda generic." The Brown-haired character then holds a ruler and a box of crayons, looking overwhelmed in an office space with bookshelves and a whiteboard covered in drawings. A male voice asks, "What was that?" and another male voice critiques, "I mean, it's corny. It doesn't hook you in. The character's motivation is unfun..." The video ends with the Brown-haired character standing in a gray, overcast cityscape, wearing a blue jacket with a fur-lined hood, looking shocked and distressed.

event_blocks.summary: The Purple-haired character, with glowing yellow eyes and a stern expression, confronts the surprised and defensive Brown-haired character, Kuramaku, in a cluttered office space featuring orange walls and a retro computer. Kuramaku, holding papers, engages with the Gray-haired character who initially praises the quality of comics, stating, "But overall, it's good. Especially compared to everyone else's comics." When asked if he's participating, Kuramaku confidently replies, "Yes, I've just finished it," to which the Gray-haired character skeptically remarks, "Ah, so that's your way of making comics. I thought you were writing a whole novel." Kuramaku excitedly gestures in front of a bulletin board covered in notes, diagrams, red string, and labels like 'TEXT', 'SPIDER-BAT', 'FUM ETTO', and '漫画', declaring, "Using statistical analysis, I have calculated the formula for the perfect comic." The Gray-haired character, unimpressed, asks, "Mmm, impressive. And what are the results?" Kuramaku admits, "I have found... nothing," prompting a dry "Now I know" from the Gray-haired character. Kuramaku then places a paper on a pink table where the Pink-haired character is drawing, who asks in a child-like voice, "Do you think maybe I could take part?" Kuramaku proudly addresses a group including the Purple-haired, Teal-haired, Yellow-haired, and Pink-haired characters, who react with shock, amusement, and concern. A gasp is heard, followed by the Gray-haired character, now sounding proud, exclaiming, "I'm proud of you guys," and joking about them not giving up before announcing, "you all actually did great! And so, the rightful winner is the comic by Spade and uh... I think we have a winner!" The Green-haired character appears behind them, holding a comic strip and smiling mischievously, as the group gathers closely. Exclamations of surprise and amazement erupt, including "What the...", "Oh!", "It's amazing!", and "What a story! And all on one page." One character questions, "Is this loss?", while another exclaims, "It's beautiful!" The Green-haired character waves dismissively, concluding the investigation with a playful and humorous shift in atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit room, a group of animated characters react with concern and surprise to an excited male voice exclaiming, "Oh! We have to show it to the world!", met with a collective "What?!" and gasps. The scene shifts to a living room where a menacing purple-haired character with glowing yellow eyes stands, causing fear in a teal-haired character and a yellow-haired character. An "Ugh!" is heard, followed by an annoyed male voice telling Charlie to watch where she's throwing, to which Charlie apologetically replies, "Oh, sorry! I don't even know how I did that!". A retro TV displays a cartoon of a gray-haired character and a yellow-haired character. Charlie, the green-haired character, leaps in excitement holding a purple object as the TV shows a cheerful yellow-haired character. An exasperated male voice questions how long they have to watch, receiving the deadpan response, "The whole season," followed by groans and a sigh. A sudden "Ah!" and a loud thud occur. On the TV, a calm male voice begins, "Good sleep is the key to...", but is cut off by an angry male voice yelling, "Just leave!". The purple-haired character is shown with a stern expression, then the group is on a pink couch, where the brown-haired character smiles confidently while others look unimpressed. Black-and-white animated credits with melancholic piano music begin, showing a sleeping character giving a thumbs-up, with text identifying production roles. The credits continue with stylized line drawings, listing voice actors, animators, and crew, including original and English voice actors, sound engineers, and background artists. The final frames display production team names with doodles, ending with the 'RESTQUE TEAM' logo and a list of background artists against a black background with white text and minimalistic character sketches.

event_blocks.summary: A black-and-white animated credit sequence unfolds with a minimalist, hand-drawn aesthetic, underscored by a continuous, gentle, melancholic piano melody. On the left, a stylized character with spiky hair and a game controller-like object leans over a small bird exhibiting a question mark, symbolizing curiosity. Concurrently, on the right, another spiky-haired character, identified as the Character Reading Book, sits cross-legged, engrossed in a book, with a thought bubble depicting a serene tropical island scene, complete with a hammock and palm tree, indicating daydreaming. As the piano plays, the Credit Sequence displays production roles and names centrally, including 'SELECTING TAKES', 'ASSISTANT CASTING DIRECTOR', 'SOUND EDITING', 'STUDIO ADMINISTRATION', and 'PRODUCERS FROM ÖKKO'. The sequence concludes with a handwritten message, 'A big thank you to the entire '13 Cards' team!', presented alongside a joyful character with curly hair and floating hearts, representing the Grateful Character with Hearts and expressing gratitude. The scene then fades to black, with the piano melody fading out.

global_entity_library.Tablet: A handheld device displaying a character customization screen with a yellow-haired girl and various options like 'Mouth' and 'Hair', held by a character in a yellow shirt.
global_entity_library.Comic strip: A black-and-white comic strip being drawn by the Green-haired character, featuring a character with a surprised expression and a speech bubble saying 'Это что, коробка с призами?' (What is this, a box with prizes?), visible in the background during the group scene.
global_entity_library.Park bench: A green wooden bench in a park, where the elderly woman sits with a pigeon perched on it, surrounded by fallen leaves and trees.
global_entity_library.Elderly woman: An elderly woman with gray hair tied in a bun, wearing a red shawl and a pink dress, holding a walking stick, sitting on a green park bench with pigeons nearby, smiling warmly.
global_entity_library.Pigeons: Two gray pigeons, one on the ground near the Brown-haired character and another on the bench beside the elderly woman, appearing in the park scene, making distinct cooing and squawking sounds.
global_entity_library.Cityscape: A gray, overcast urban environment with tall buildings, visible in the background as the Brown-haired character walks through the city, looking worried, as critical dialogue about storytelling plays out.
global_entity_library.Pink-haired character: A character with pink hair, wearing a yellow shirt, who appears in the group with a shocked expression, standing beside the Yellow-haired character.
global_entity_library.Bulletin board: A green-framed board covered in papers, diagrams, red string, and notes, including labels like 'TEXT', 'SPIDER-BAT', 'FUM ETTO', and a yellow sticky note with Chinese characters '漫画' (màn huà, meaning 'comic'), serving as the central focus of the investigation.
global_entity_library.Pink table: A pink table where the Pink-haired character is drawing, and where the Brown-haired character places a paper, serving as a key piece of furniture in the office space.
global_entity_library.Red string: Red string connecting various notes and diagrams on the bulletin board, used to illustrate connections between clues in the investigation, adding a visual element of mystery and deduction.
global_entity_library.Gray-haired character: A character with short gray hair, wearing a white shirt and black tie, who appears on the TV screen, holding a pen and listening skeptically.
global_entity_library.Credit Sequence: A black-and-white animated sequence featuring stylized line drawings of characters and text listing production roles, accompanied by a gentle, melancholic piano melody throughout.
global_entity_library.Sleeping Character Sketch: A gray-scale sketch of a character with spiky hair sleeping in bed, then sitting up and giving a thumbs-up, used in the opening credits to represent the director and author.
global_entity_library.RESTQUE TEAM Logo: A stylized logo with the text 'RESTQUE TEAM' in bold, white letters, appearing at the end of the credits sequence, accompanied by a list of background artists and character doodles, all set to the melancholic piano music.
global_entity_library.Grateful Character with Hearts: A small, joyful character with curly hair, floating hearts around it, appearing below the thank-you message, expressing appreciation.
global_entity_library.Character Reading Book: A character with spiky hair sitting cross-legged, reading a book, with a thought bubble showing a tropical island, indicating daydreaming.
global_entity_library.Character with Game Controller: A stylized character with spiky hair holding a game controller-like object, leaning over a small bird with a question mark, suggesting curiosity or confusion.
global_entity_library.Thought Bubble with Tropical Scene: A cloud-shaped thought bubble above a character, depicting a tropical island with a hammock, palm tree, and sun, symbolizing daydreaming or relaxation.
